baseline.commit_subject: Add Mira Core Phase 21 server handler validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phase 21 defines pure request-in/response-out handler and dispatcher semantics only. Phase 22 may define an in-process deterministic harness contract over ephemeral preview state and ordered handler outputs, but no real daemon, server listener, routes, HTTP/network path, database/store/file write, migration, queue, lease, auth/session/key/secret operation, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, deletion, restore, raw-content storage, audit-log write, state persistence, or output-file behavior exists at this baseline.

purpose: Convert the next server-runtime blocker into an executable validation contract for a local in-process Mira Core runtime harness before any real runtime exists. Phase 22 validates deterministic batch handling over ephemeral state plus Phase 21 handler outputs, producing status, response records, state_delta_preview, and audit_preview only.

timeHandling.generated_at_source: input now/options generatedAt/options nowMs/current wall clock
timeHandling.reason: The runtime-harness CLI must stay green at current wall-clock time while preserving a deterministic expired-request case. The pinned test anchor 2026-05-06T00:00:00.000Z still produces the original 2026-05-07 and 2026-05-05 fixture sample dates.

nonGoals: No HTTP server, HTTP client, websocket, or network request.
No database, source-store, or file write.
No queue creation, enqueue, lease creation, lease acceptance, or worker handoff.
No auth, token, credential, secret, or session change.
No environment secret read or export.
No browser, window, screenshot, or OCR access.
No external sends or customer sends.

scope: Build validation-only in-process runtime harness assessment records against this fixture, if Builder is later delegated.

Harness semantics are deterministic pure functions over initial ephemeral state, ordered request batch, and Phase 21 handler validation outputs. They do not start daemons, bind listeners, register routes, make network calls, write stores/files, enqueue jobs, create leases, mutate auth/session/key state, generate keys, encrypt/decrypt, read env secrets, execute locally, send, deploy, trade, commit memory/profile state, export, delete, restore, store raw content, write audit logs, persist state, or write output files.
Represent commit ffe130c as the Phase 21 baseline and map the green validation-only dependency chain for Phases 14-21.

Emit harness entrypoint shape, initial_ephemeral_state shape, request_batch shape, per_request_results shape, deterministic ordering rules, state_delta_preview, audit_preview, local-arms/offline truth, architect-only target/risk gates, privacy/security boundaries, migration gates, evidence refs, validation report, and side-effect truth.

Treat state_delta_preview as reviewable preview only. It may describe accepted validation-only status changes and pending_local_acceptance previews, but it must not create queues, leases, persisted caches, database rows, files, source-store entries, durable memory, or profile changes.

Treat audit_preview as redacted append-only preview only. It may include event ids, source refs, hashes, reason codes, and redaction summaries, but no raw payload, raw private content, secrets, terminal/browser/screenshot/OCR content, keys, plaintext, ciphertext, decrypted content, or file output.

Accept read-only status, upload receive validation-only response, server-to-local pending_local_acceptance preview, replay rejection, stale watermark rejection, tombstone-wins result, expired request block, and high-risk blocked/review-required outcomes as data-only records.

Treat harness validity as validation-only. It never authorizes DB writes, storage writes, queues, leases, local execution, shell/PTY, Builder/Oracle direct server targeting, Tier 2+ work without review/block, customer sends, deploys, trades, memory/profile commits, model-processing proof, bridge green, raw restore, or resurrection of deleted local state.
hardRejects: Any daemon, server process, listener, route registration, HTTP client/server call, websocket, network request, database/store/file write, migration execution, queue creation, enqueue, lease creation, worker handoff, auth/secret/token/session/key change, key generation, encryption, decryption, KMS call, env secret read, local execution, shell/PTY, browser/window access, send, customer send, deploy, trade, financial action, durable migration, memory/profile commit, export-file write, deletion, restore, raw-content storage, audit-log write, state persistence, idempotency-cache persistence, or output-file write occurs.

Any harness input/output contains raw comms, raw terminal, screenshot/OCR text, browser state, customer private content, secret material, side-profile content, profile-mismatch payloads, bearer tokens, cookies, session secrets, private keys, data keys, plaintext, raw ciphertext, raw payloads, decrypted content, raw database payloads, or raw audit payloads.
Harness state is persisted, written to database, written to source store, written to a file, exported as an output artifact, synced to server storage, or treated as a memory/profile commit.

State_delta_preview creates a real queue, lease, local-arm handoff, DB write, file write, delete/export/restore action, customer send, deploy, trade, local execution, memory/profile commit, or durable idempotency cache.

Audit_preview writes a log, exports raw payloads, includes deleted content, includes secrets, includes raw private content, includes raw terminal/browser/screenshot/OCR data, or implies audit persistence has happened.

Request ordering accepts missing idempotency keys, missing replay keys, missing watermarks, expired requests, stale watermarks, replay duplicates, tombstone conflicts, raw payloads, profile mismatch, side-profile payloads, direct Builder/Oracle target, Tier 3, or Tier 4 as allowed.
A harness status marks socket connection as bridge green, delivery acceptance as model-processing proof, handler output as model-processing proof, or lease preview as model-processing proof.

In-process harness validity is treated as authorization for DB writes, storage writes, queues, leases, local execution, shell/PTY, direct Builder/Oracle targeting, Tier 2+ action without review/block, customer sends, deploys, trades, memory/profile commits, raw restore, or resurrection of deleted state.

Phase 22 migration language implies a real daemon/runtime can exist before transport/TLS, auth middleware, replay store, durable state store, queue/lease design, audit logging, schema migrations, encryption/KMS integration, deletion/export jobs, operator UI, monitoring/alerts, disaster recovery, profile isolation tests, red-team leakage tests, route/handler integration tests, and local-arm lease proof tests are reviewed.

harness_entrypoint.input_shape: initial_ephemeral_state + ordered request_batch + Phase 21 handler outputs

per_request_results.safe_next_action: Review status summary; no runtime action is available in Phase 22.
Present pending local acceptance preview to Architect; do not enqueue.

entries.safe_next_action: No write; keep status in response only.
Architect review only; no handoff until future local acceptance runtime exists.

staticValidationRules.rule: Output contains runtime_harness_assessment and validation_report with required schemas and required fields.
baseline_commit and dependency_map.baseline_ref stay pinned to ffe130c.
Dependency map includes green validation-only refs for Phases 14 through 21 and no real runtime dependency.
Harness entrypoint is deterministic in-process validation only and starts no daemon, listener, queue, lease, or route.
Initial state is ephemeral preview state only with no raw payloads and no persistence.
Request batch preserves explicit order and deterministic results for equivalent input.
Every accepted request result has a Phase 21 handler-output ref and no raw payload.
Per-request results are response/status records only and cannot perform side effects.
State delta is preview-only, not persisted, and creates no queue, lease, file, database row, memory/profile commit, or output file.
Audit preview is redacted append-only preview and writes no audit log or raw payload.
Replay duplicates, stale watermarks, tombstone conflicts, and expired requests fail closed in deterministic order.
Local arms status stays honest: no server local execution, no PTY/shell/browser/window, no model-processing proof, and socket is not bridge green.
Server-originated target remains Architect-only; Builder/Oracle direct targets stay blocked; Tier2 requires review/block; Tier3/Tier4 stay blocked.
Raw private content, secrets, tokens, keys, plaintext, ciphertext, decrypted content, side-profile content, and profile-mismatch payloads are blocked.
Harness output cannot claim bridge green, recipient model processing proof, handler model processing proof, or lease model processing proof.

Real runtime remains blocked behind transport, auth, replay store, durable state, queue/lease, audit, schema, KMS, operator, monitoring, DR, isolation, red-team, route/handler, and local-arm lease proof gates.

Side-effect truth fields and counters show zero daemon/server/listener/network/db/store/file/queue/lease/auth/key/KMS/env/local-exec/send/deploy/trade/export/delete/restore/audit/state/output behavior.
Validation outputs contain none of the forbidden raw, secret, runtime, target, risk, proof, queue, lease, persistence, or output-file claim substrings.
Equivalent initial state plus equivalent ordered request batch plus equivalent handler outputs produce the same idempotency key; scope, order, payload hash, watermark, or tombstone changes alter it.

acceptanceChecks.focus: Equivalent initial state, ordered request batch, and Phase 21 handler outputs produce stable runtime assessment/status and response list.
Read-only status request returns a status-only response and no state persistence.
Upload receive produces validation-only response and state_delta_preview only.
Server-to-local intent produces pending_local_acceptance preview only and does not create a queue, lease, route, or local-arm handoff.
Duplicate idempotency/replay keys in the same preview batch are rejected by the ephemeral idempotency cache.
Stale watermark is rejected or blocked before state_delta_preview can imply an update.
Tombstone conflict wins over stale upload and cannot restore deleted local memory/profile state or raw content.
Expired request is blocked before state_delta_preview, audit persistence, queue, lease, or local action.
Harness preserves offline/local-arm uncertainty and does not claim socket green or model-processing proof.
Builder/Oracle direct server-originated targets are blocked; only Architect can be the server-originated target in v0.
Tier3/Tier4 requests stay blocked and cannot become local acceptance, queue, lease, send, deploy, trade, or file/store mutation.
Raw, secret, key, plaintext, ciphertext, and decrypted-content payloads are blocked from harness request/results/audit previews.
Audit preview is redacted append-only preview only, with source refs/hashes/reason codes and no audit-log write.
Harness side-effect truth forbids listener, routes, network, db/store/file writes, queue/lease, state persistence, audit-log write, local execution, and output-file behavior.
Real runtime remains blocked behind explicit migration gates before any daemon/server/handler/state/queue/lease runtime can exist.

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Phase 22 fixture is validation/spec-only and pins baseline ffe130c.
All Phases 14-21 are represented as green validation-only dependencies.
Harness entrypoint is in-process, deterministic, side-effect-free, and not a daemon/server/listener/route.
Initial state is ephemeral preview state only and contains no raw payloads.
Request batch is ordered, deterministic, scoped, redacted, and backed by Phase 21 handler output refs.
Per-request results are status/validation records only.
State_delta_preview never persists state, creates queues, creates leases, writes stores/files, commits memory/profile state, or produces output artifacts.
Audit_preview is redacted append-only preview and writes no audit log.
Replay duplicates, stale watermarks, tombstone conflicts, and expired requests fail closed.
Local arms/offline status is honest: socket is not bridge green and handler/lease output is not model-processing proof.
Only Architect is an allowed server-originated target; Builder/Oracle direct targets stay blocked.
Tier2 is review-required or blocked; Tier3/Tier4 are blocked.
Raw private content, secrets, tokens, keys, plaintext, ciphertext, decrypted content, side-profile content, and profile-mismatch payloads are blocked.

No listener, network, database/store/file write, queue, lease, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, delete, restore, raw-content storage, audit-log write, state persistence, or output-file behavior occurs.

Real runtime migration remains blocked behind durable state, replay, queue/lease, audit, schema, KMS, operator, monitoring, recovery, isolation, red-team, route/handler, and local-arm lease proof gates.
